summary: A Type II error occurs when a researcher fails to detect a true difference between groups, leading to incorrect conclusions that no effect exists when one genuinely does. In clinical contexts, this can result in missed opportunities for implementing effective treatments due to insufficient study power or poor trial design. \boxed{B}

In clinical contexts, this can result in missed opportunities for implementing effective treatments due to insufficient study power or poor trial design. \boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
